forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over NSW will continue to move eastwards to be centred over the Tasman Sea by Monday. Northerly winds freshen over the coastal waters on Tuesday ahead of a weakening cold front mid-week, with a second cold front expected late in the week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: Northeasterly 15 to 20 knots.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Clear.
North to northeasterly 15 to 25 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Sunny.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 20 to 25 knots before dawn then tending north to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the late afternoon and evening.
Southwesterly 15 to 25 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the morning.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres inshore, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres offshore.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: Northerly 10 to 15 knots turning northeasterly in the late afternoon. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the late evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Clear.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Upper West Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Streaky Bay in the late evening.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Sunny.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning west to northwesterly in the evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm offshore in the evening.
West to southwesterly 15 to 25 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the late evening.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Clear.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Sunny.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the evening.
Westerly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning then becoming north to northwesterly during the evening.
2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres later in the evening.
Clear.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Monday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots increasing to 15 to 25 knots in the morning. Winds reaching up to 30 knots in the west in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Sunny.
Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the evening.
2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the west in the evening.
Westerly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to 15 to 20 knots during the morning then becoming northwesterly below 10 knots during the evening.
2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending westerly 3 to 5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
